mechanism_narrative: A rapid, large increase in the unemployment rate over a short period signals a severe economic contraction is underway. This leads to sharp downward revisions of corporate earnings forecasts and causes negative equity returns as investors de-risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 1.0 percentage point over 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:Lehman Brothers bankruptcy in Sep 2008 caused a sharp economic deterioration.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event during NBER-dated recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decrease in the effective federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary easing. This lowers the discount rate for future corporate cash flows and stimulates risk appetite, leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve cut the federal funds rate multiple times in 2008 in response to the crisis.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative (i.e., p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous year-over-year decline in both consumer spending (retail sales) and industrial production indicates a synchronized collapse in both demand and supply-side activity. This feedback loop signals a deep recession, leading to sharply lower earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year percentage change in both RSAFS and INDPRO are negative.
evidence_refs: fact:The Global Financial Crisis involved a broad-based collapse in economic activity.
failure_condition: When both series show year-over-year declines, the S&P 500 has positive 60-day forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production, measured by its 6-month momentum, reflects contracting manufacturing and business activity. This directly implies lower corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:NBER recession dating relies heavily on production and income data.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Large negative monthly changes in nonfarm payrolls confirm widespread job destruction, which severely curtails aggregate consumer demand and signals deep corporate distress. This effect is most pronounced during officially declared recessions, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Massive job losses were a defining feature of the 2008-2009 recession.
failure_condition: During recessions, following a monthly PAYEMS drop of over 100k, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed is cutting rates but inflation is also falling rapidly (disinflation), the decline in real interest rates is muted, limiting the stimulative effect on equities. The positive impact of a FEDFUNDS cut is therefore weaker when CPIAUCSL momentum is also negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Concerns about a deflationary spiral emerged in late 2008 as commodity prices collapsed.
failure_condition: A regression of forward returns on FEDFUNDS changes shows no statistically significant negative interaction term with CPIAUCSL changes (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in new residential construction (housing starts) below a critical long-term support level indicates a severe contraction in a key economic sector, with negative spillovers to consumer wealth, banking, and employment, driving negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level falls below 1,000 thousands of units.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2008 crisis originated in the subprime housing market.
failure_condition: After HOUST falls below the 1,000k threshold, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate increases the probability of aggressive monetary easing by the Federal Reserve. When the Fed Funds rate is not yet at the zero lower bound, markets price in these expected rate cuts, leading to positive short-term equity returns as 'bad news is good news'.
evidence_refs: fact:Markets anticipate Fed actions based on its dual mandate of price stability and maximum employment.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is rising and FEDFUNDS is above the zero lower bound, the S&P 500 has negative 5-day forward returns more than 50% of the time.
